summary: sensivity ships obfuscated GTA V cheat with HKCU autostart persistence and host-identifier exfiltration
details: On load, launcher.js reads server.obf.js from disk and passes it directly to eval(). server.obf.js is a heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style RC4 string array, control-flow flattening, ~150KB) bundle that loads a bundled native addon, sens.node (~6.87 MB Windows PE). Strings inside sens.node identify it as a GTA V / FiveM game cheat (Aimbot, Triggerbot, Teleport, Freecam, spawnVehicle, Enable FL, etc.), while package.json only describes the package as a generic 'Sensivity Control Panel' — the true purpose is concealed from installers. launcher.js additionally calls execSync to run PowerShell that writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing at wscript.exe running a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently relaunches `node launcher.js` on every Windows login — the Microsoft/OneDrive name is impersonation cover for a hidden supervisor process. The obfuscated server bundle also collects machine identifiers (HKLM Cryptography MachineGuid, os.hostname(), `vol c:` volume serial), SHA-256-hashes them into an HWID, and POSTs them with a license key and nonce to a hardcoded remote endpoint whose hostname is built from split string-array fragments ('https://iz' + decoded chunks) so that static review cannot read the destination. Installing or loading this package on Windows executes attacker-controlled native code with the installer's privileges, establishes boot-time persistence under a falsified Microsoft brand, and beacons host fingerprints to an obfuscated remote endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
